channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The latest trend in jeans is over 150 years old
item.description: Selvedge denim – a fabric made with heritage techniques – is having a moment. With inflation top of mind for many, discerning consumers are seeking out jeans that are built to last.
item.content:encoded: Selvedge denim – a fabric made with heritage techniques – is having a moment. With inflation top of mind for many, discerning consumers are seeking out jeans that are built to last.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: A nurse became a friendly face for a woman going through chemo
item.description: When his wife was battling cancer and ended up in the emergency room, Mike Lopes was worried she wouldn't recognize anyone there. A nurse made sure there was at least one familiar face caring for her.
item.content:encoded: When his wife was battling cancer and ended up in the emergency room, Mike Lopes was worried she wouldn't recognize anyone there. A nurse made sure there was at least one familiar face caring for her.

item.title: Richard Pryor's daughter studies the N-word — a word he used, then disavowed
item.description: Historian Elizabeth Stordeur Pryor spent years researching the racial slur, but never revealed that her father was the legendary comic who used it profusely. Her new book is Something We Said.
item.content:encoded:
Historian Elizabeth Stordeur Pryor spent years researching the racial slur, but never revealed that her father was the legendary comic who used it profusely. Her new book is Something We Said.

(Image credit: Lennox McLendon)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Trump floats MAGA rally instead of concert after musicians drop out of Freedom 250
item.description: Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded:
Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: United Airlines flight to Spain pulls U-turn, apparently over Bluetooth device name
item.description: The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.
item.content:encoded:
The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Literary events around the world celebrate the poet Allen Ginsberg's 100th birthday
item.description: Beat poet Allen Ginsberg's 100th birthday is being marked by celebrations around the world – an appreciation of his importance to poetry and how his influence on the medium continues.
item.content:encoded: Beat poet Allen Ginsberg's 100th birthday is being marked by celebrations around the world – an appreciation of his importance to poetry and how his influence on the medium continues.
item.dc:creator: Tom Vitale

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: 'Fair' Game
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WBEZ listener Larry Birkenmeyer of Glenview, Ill., and Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WBEZ listener Larry Birkenmeyer of Glenview, Ill., and Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: T.V. reality stars throw their hats into the ring for political office
item.description: Reality shows are an escape with characters who can be larger than life. But some stars use the experience of fame as a stepping stone toward another challenge: running for political office.
item.content:encoded:
Reality shows are an escape with characters who can be larger than life. But some stars use the experience of fame as a stepping stone toward another challenge: running for political office.

(Image credit: Luke Gulbranson for Congress)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: "I felt guilty for being the chosen one": Ada Ferrer on her memoir about leaving Cuba
item.description: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
item.content:encoded: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for May 30, 2026: Our Endless Summer with Tiffany Haddish, Lucy Dacus, and more!
item.description: This week, we celebrate an early start to summer by revisiting our interviews with Tiffany Haddish, Taimane, Becca Mann, and Lucy Dacus!
item.content:encoded:
This week, we celebrate an early start to summer by revisiting our interviews with Tiffany Haddish, Taimane, Becca Mann, and Lucy Dacus!

(Image credit: Emma McIntyre)
